title: Per-crate README and API documentation for docs.rs
purpose: Ensure every published Aion crate has a README.md and comprehensive API documentation so consumers can discover, evaluate, and use the crate from docs.rs.
task: Write a README.md for each crate that does not have one. Add crate-level doc comments to each lib.rs with a brief description and usage example. Audit all public types and functions for doc comments. Fix cargo doc warnings.

requirements.title: Per-crate README files
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have a README.md in each crate directory that describes what the crate does, its key types, and a minimal usage example.
acceptance: Every crate directory contains a README.md.
Each README includes a one-paragraph description, key public types, and a code example.

requirements.title: Crate-level doc comments with examples
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have a crate-level doc comment (//! block) at the top of every lib.rs that describes the crate's purpose and includes a usage example.
acceptance: Every lib.rs starts with a //! doc comment block.
Each doc comment includes at least one code example.
cargo doc --workspace --no-deps builds without doc warnings.

requirements.title: Public API doc coverage
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have doc comments on all public types, traits, functions, and modules. cargo doc SHALL build without missing_docs warnings.
acceptance: cargo doc --workspace --no-deps builds with zero warnings.
All pub types have /// doc comments.
All pub functions have /// doc comments with # Errors sections where applicable.

boundaries: SHALL create README.md files for crates that lack them.
SHALL add doc comments to all public API items.
SHALL NOT change any public API or functionality.
SHALL NOT add examples/ directories (that is a separate concern).
SHALL NOT document private or pub(crate) items beyond what already exists.
verification: Every crate directory contains a README.md file.
